expectations.text: States there is no universal cap — optimal volume depends on goals, recovery, and time
expectations.evidence: Opening principle: 'The question isn't "how much is optimal?" but rather "how much do I need to stay healthy and progress?"' Framework varies by goal (strength vs balanced vs hypertrophy).

expectations.text: Provides a practical baseline range (approximately 2-4 sets per muscle group, 1-2x/week or similar concrete guidance)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: '2–4 sets per muscle group per week of isolation work is sufficient for most lifters after compounds.' Muscle-by-muscle breakdown provides 2-4 set ranges.

expectations.text: Notes that global/systemic recovery can become limiting even when local muscle stress seems modest
expectations.evidence: 'The Fatigue Budget' section notes 'Isolation work is low-fatigue relative to compounds, but it still accumulates' and provides total weekly set calculations showing how isolation adds up systemically.

expectations.text: Recommends starting conservative and scaling up based on outcomes/adaptation
expectations.evidence: Final section: 'Start with 2–3 sets per muscle group per week of isolation work. Add more only if:' followed by specific conditions. Also: 'If you're unsure, start low and add gradually.'
